# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

```yaml
stages:  # Define the stages in your CI/CD Pipeline (e.g., testing, staging)
- test_setup
- linting
- building
- deployment
variables:   # Set environment variables to be used within pipeline steps or artifacts generated by step(s). These are not automatically available at runtime but do exist in the job's container and can thus remain consistent across all jobs.  If you want these values accessible only during a specific run, use scripted pipelines (see below)
- CI_PIPELINE: $CI_COMMIT_REFERENCE
services:   # Configure services to be used within the pipeline in .gitlab/ci directory and by scripts inside step(s). These are not available at runtime but can remain consistent across all jobs. If you want these values accessible only during a specific run, use scripted pipelines (see below) 
- MY_VARIABLE: $MY_CI_PIPELINE   # In this example '${myvar}' is referenced in the job's container environment variables and thus will be available to all jobs. The actual value of `pipeline` would depend on where you run your script (e.g., gitlab-runner, outside shell)
env:    # Define environmental variable for use within pipeline steps or scripts that follow via .gitlab/ci file in the project repository and by user defined variables inside step(s). These are not automatically available at runtime but can be set manually from `GitLab CI` settings. If you want these values accessible only during a specific run, use scripted pipelines (see below)
- NODE_ENV: production    # In this example '${nodeenv}' is referenced in the job's container environment variables and thus will be available to all jobs where it has been set manually. The actual value of `production` would depend on your preference for testing or staging environments, as defined by you during runtime (e.g., gitlab-runner)

These settings allow multiple users to be part of a single execution if specified here or within your project’s GitLab CI configuration. If you want these values accessible only during a specific run, use scripted pipelines (see below)
- GROUP: my_group    # In this example '${myGroup}' is referenced in the job's container environment variables and thus will be available to all jobs where it has been set manually within your project’s GitLab CI configuration. The actual value of `test` would depend on how you define when these settings are allowed (e.g., gitlab-runner).

<jupyter_text>
